title: Asynchronous Architecture Decisions for Go Services
description: A Go 1.26 service team must make coupling, buffering, fan-out, replay, and failure costs observable at Go service boundaries; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. Learners cumulatively produce a Go service coupling and broker-fit decision record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
